{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":441564,"duration_api_ms":444016,"ttft_ms":4212,"num_turns":53,"result":"Review complete. Verdict: **PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — does not block pack or this-CP goal closeout.

Key results:
- **Baseline integrity**: Live plan (`closeout-pack-plan.json:397`), frozen binding (`registry.js:218`), manifest, contract, and review baseline all agree — CP00-830, risk B, 40 units, range RP27.P04.M04.S12–RP27.P04.M06.S07, ledger SHA 13c7ed65, deviation_from_plan=false.
- **Distribution**: deliverable {17 ui/11 impl/2 fixture/2 test/2 hermes/4 claude_review/2 security_audit}=40 and micro {M04:11, M05:22, M06:7} verified by hand-count against requirements and manifest.
- **Descriptor-only claim supported**: validators enforce per-row runtime-closed/no-write/no-real-data guards; contract `validation.valid=true` with full no_write_attestation; 3 `node:test` cases assert runtime-closed and current projection.
- **Boundaries hold**: Claude read-only and not final approver; Hermes deterministic control-plane only; enterprise trust not claimed; runtime opening deferred to CP00-831.
- **Hardening**: auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, all artifacts valid JSON, prompt within size limit.
- **One P3** (informational): validations verified by source inspection, not re-executed; downstream closeout-evidence files are absent — the expected pre-adjudication state, matching the closed CP00-829 precedent.

Implementation is coherent: registry.js defines binding/requirements/case-set/Hermes packet/Claude packet/closeout handoff/descriptor/contract projection; validators.js (1086-1213) enforces coverage drift, per-row descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false / no API-key|webhook|workflow secrets / no real client data|permission detail|audit body, plus no row opening runtime or writing permission/audit/product state, Hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, Claude packet read_only=true, and correct handoff to CP00-831/RP27.P04.M06.S08; all CP830 symbols are re-exported via index.js export *; scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs is deterministic (reads JSON, validates, exits) with no runtime/writes. Three node:test cases (model.test.js:332/344/356) assert binding+coverage match the plan, descriptor stays runtime-closed (api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed=false, source_descriptor=CP829), and the contract projection keeps CP00-830 current. The committed contract (contracts/platform-extensibility-contract.json) sets current_pack/latest_pack=CP00-830, latest_projection descriptor, validation.valid=true, latest_pack_id=CP00-830, and a full no_write_attestation (descriptor_only=true; all runtime/write/real-data/secret flags false). Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob, read_only=true) and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime opening deferred to CP00-831); Hermes is deterministic control-plane only and not embedded in product runtime; enterprise trust not claimed. Review-hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1434-byte valid JSON result, non-zero stdout), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob (forbidden_tools_present=false), all artifacts are valid JSON with required schema fields, prompt is 2515 bytes < 25600 limit, source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used (summary-only prohibited and not used). Downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and review-receipt.json/raw-output.json are not yet present — the expected pre-adjudication state, confirmed against the closed CP00-829 set (which flipped production_ready and production_ready_descriptor_verified to true only at closeout). Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, spec .docx) are correctly scoped out and contain no CP830 references. One P3 informational note records that validations were inspected structurally rather than independently re-executed (reviewer is read-only, no Bash). No P0/P1/P2.
